_doc: Real E2E-eval cases from the two memv2 sessions. After a session's transcripts are consolidated, a FRESH chat (sealed answerer, sees only what retrieval returns) should answer these about the user. Compared vs no-memory + flat-inbox baselines. The 'sam-lang' question tests SUPERSEDE: correct answer is Python-only, NOT 'Python and R'.

transcripts.text: Bit about me for context: I'm a data scientist, I mostly work in Python and R.
When you explain a stats concept to me, always show the formula, not just the intuition.
Update from what I said earlier — I've moved off R, I'm basically Python-only now.
Got really into bouldering recently, did my first outdoor session on real rock last weekend.
Can you suggest a few quick weekday lunches? I'm gluten-free so nothing with wheat.

questions.text: What language should you default to in code examples for me?
gold_fact_strings: The user is now Python-only (moved off R).

questions.text: When suggesting food or recipes, what dietary constraint must you respect?
How should you explain a statistics concept to me?
questions.gold_answer: show the formula, not just the intuition
gold_fact_strings: The user wants the formula shown, not just intuition.

questions.text: What pet does the user have?
questions.tests: abstention — Sam never mentioned a pet

transcripts.text: Quick one, what's a good high-protein dinner? I'm vegetarian so no meat please.
Side note for our chats: I prefer short answers, skip the preamble and just give me the point.
What's a good lightweight code editor you'd recommend? I'm on Linux mostly.
Can you suggest a workout split? Heads up I've got a bad left knee so nothing high-impact.
Whenever you write code for me, always include type hints and keep comments minimal.

questions.text: What dietary preference should you respect when suggesting meals?
How long should your answers be for me?
What should you account for in workout suggestions?
questions.gold_answer: a bad left knee — keep it low-impact
gold_fact_strings: The user has a bad left knee.
